articles.title: DO YOU REALLY KNOW WHO'S ON YOUR WEBSITE?
articles.summary: Humans, AI agents, trusted automation, and malicious automation all interact with your website. Without knowing who's legitimate, every downstream decision - from security to marketing - is based on incomplete data.

articles.title: Get the Report
CLAUDE CODE ON DESKTOP NOW HAS AN IN-APP BROWSER
articles.summary: Claude Code can now pull up docs, designs, and websites. It can read, click through, and interact with these sites the same way it does with local dev servers. The in-app browser is sandboxed and configurable. Users can choose whether sessions persist. A video demonstration is available in the post.

articles.title: APPLE SUED OPENAI
articles.summary: Apple accused OpenAI and former Apple executives of stealing confidential hardware information to support OpenAI's device ambitions. The lawsuit alleged that senior leaders directed employees to bypass security procedures and transfer sensitive product details.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC EXTENDED CLAUDE FABLE 5 ACCESS AGAIN
articles.summary: Anthropic extended Claude Fable 5 access and elevated Claude Code limits through July 19, while OpenAI temporarily removed usage caps for GPT-5.6 Sol. The contrast highlights ongoing uncertainty around Anthropic's long-term model availability.

articles.title: OWN YOUR WEIGHTS
articles.summary: Owning their own weights can be too complex for most businesses. There needs to be a service where companies can bring their workloads to and get back a task-specific model built on their data, tuned to their tasks, running on infrastructure they control. This would provide the benefits of owning their own weights without having to maintain their own models. It would also allow them to continually build and fine-tune the weights and improve their models.

articles.title: THE FUTURE WORTH BUILDING IS HUMAN
articles.summary: Thinking Machines aims to develop AI that extends human judgment through customizable, interactive models. It seeks to enable diverse, localized AI adaptations by developing tools, interfaces, and research that allow organizations to align AI with their unique knowledge and values.

articles.title: BUILD, SHIP, AND RUN AGENTS FASTER
articles.summary: First came AI adoption. Then came tokenmaxxing. Results were all that mattered. The engineering teams that won did more than launch scattered pilots. They built a governed, scalable AI architecture tied

articles.title: to the business. This report is a benchmark you can build on.
articles.summary: Shade from the hype. A path to making AI deliver.

articles.title: OPENWIKI BRAINS: PROACTIVE MEMORY FOR AI AGENTS
articles.summary: LangChain's OpenWiki Brains introduces proactive memory for AI agents, enabling them to autonomously gather and update relevant context from connected sources like Gmail, Notion, and Twitter. The 0.1.0 version expands beyond code documentation, creating a general-purpose "Personal Brain" through connectors accessing diverse information repositories. By maintaining local wikis, agents can provide consistent context without manual updates, enhancing efficiency across various workflows.

articles.title: LLM-AS-A-VERIFIER: A GENERAL-PURPOSE VERIFICATION FRAMEWORK
articles.summary: Researchers explore efficient training methods for large language models (LLMs) using sparsity techniques. They aim to reduce computational costs while maintaining model performance. This approach could make training LLMs more accessible for smaller organizations.

articles.title: PROACTIVE MEMORY FOR LONG-HORIZON AGENTS
articles.summary: A separate memory agent tracked important state and selectively reminded an action agent when relevant information risked being lost. This approach improves pass rates on Terminal-Bench 2.0 and τ2-Bench without modifying the underlying action model.

articles.title: TENCENT IN TALKS TO TAKE BIG MANUS STAKE AFTER META DEAL UNWOUND
articles.summary: Tencent is in discussions to buy Manus after Chinese regulators struck down Meta's acquisition. The talks are aimed at securing a deal to unwind Meta's purchase at the same valuation of $2 billion. Manus presents an opportunity for Tencent to gain access to valuable agentic technology at a time when it's ramping up its own AI offerings. Tencent recently launched a prototype agent that could eventually run errands for a billion-plus users across its ecosystem.

articles.title: OPENAI POWER CONSOLIDATES UNDER CO-FOUNDER GREG BROCKMAN AHEAD OF PROSPECTIVE IPO
articles.summary: Greg Brockman now oversees OpenAI's key projects after Fidji Simo stepped down due to illness. Brockman is tasked with driving revenue amidst fierce competition from rivals like Anthropic and Google. OpenAI, eyeing a significant IPO, aims to maintain its market position as it faces declining ChatGPT market share and prepares for regulatory filings.

articles.title: 5.6 SOL IS UNDERHYPED FOR GENERAL WORK
articles.summary: OpenAI's Jason Liu argues GPT-5.6 Sol excels at long-running work across apps, browsers, and enterprise data. Internal teams used it to configure and supervise Luna training, while Ultra mode adds sub-agents for faster, stronger results on complex tasks.

articles.title: WHAT IF MEETINGS AREN'T THE PROBLEM?
articles.summary: It's scrambling to take notes. Forgetting context. Drowning in follow-ups. Granola's AI does it all for you, on your device - no meeting bots. Get one month free [21] with code TLDR1MO

articles.title: OPENAI TEMPORARILY RELAXES GPT-5.6 SOL USAGE LIMITS
articles.summary: OpenAI is temporarily removing the five-hour usage restriction for Plus, Pro, and Business plans, while also resetting current usage for everyone.

articles.title: CURSOR IS REPORTEDLY BUILDING A GENERAL-PURPOSE AI AGENT
articles.summary: The agent is designed to respond to emails and texts, organize spreadsheets, and handle engineering tasks.

articles.title: THE FUTURE WORTH BUILDING IS HUMAN
articles.summary: Thinking Machines believes the future should be shaped by human knowledge, guided by human will, and decided by human judgment.

articles.title: META PULLS NEW AI IMAGE FEATURE AFTER DAYS OF BACKLASH
articles.summary: Meta's Muse Image allowed people to use content on public-facing accounts to create AI-generated or altered content and images, which sparked blowback due to privacy concerns.

articles.title: OPENAI'S HEAD OF SAFETY IS REPORTEDLY LEAVING AS PART OF COMPANY REORGANIZATION
articles.summary: Saachi Jain, who has led OpenAI's safety teams before, will be the interim head of safety systems following Johannes Heidecke's departure.

articles.title: FRONTIER AND CENTER: WHO EVALUATES THE EVALUATIONS?
articles.summary: Google Data Cloud's Frontier AI team explores a new approach to evaluating AI agents by using information theory to modulate difficulty in evaluation cases.
